title: Sauvetrea napoensis
taxonomy: Sauvetrea napoensis belongs to the family Vittariaceae and is a species of fern in the genus Sauvetrea. It is a type of climbing fern that is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The classification of Sauvetrea napoensis is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This classification reflects the plant's unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the fern group. The classification of Sauvetrea napoensis is an important aspect of understanding its biology and ecological role in its native habitat.
